qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary SQL execution via backup restore permits PHP file write and RCE
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes Zip Slip path traversal via extractTo() without entry-name validation, allowing files to be written outside writable/uploads/. The finding describes SQL injection via arbitrary SQL execution from extracted .sql files. These are different root causes: path traversal (CWE-22) vs SQL injection (CWE-89). The vulnerable code locations differ: the advisory identifies lines 100-114 in Backup::restore() where extractTo() is called, while the finding identifies DbBackup::restore() executing SQL statements.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described Zip Slip vulnerability. The advisory describes CWE-22 path traversal via ZipArchive::extractTo() in Backup::restore() at modules/Backup/Controllers/Backup.php lines 100-114, where ZIP entry names like ../../public/shell.php escape writable/uploads/. The backup_restore_sql_rce finding describes SQL injection via DbBackup::restore() executing arbitrary SQL statements, which is a different vulnerability (CWE-89). The fileeditor_auth_bypass finding is non-qualifying (requires active user interaction).
evidence: Advisory root cause: ZipArchive::extractTo($uploadPath) at Backup.php:108 without validating entry names resolves outside the intended directory
backup_restore_sql_rce finding root cause: DbBackup::restore() executes arbitrary SQL from extracted .sql file
backup_restore_sql_rce finding vulnerability class: SQL injection (CWE-89), not path traversal
